block_01: An RSI trading strategy using the Relative Strength Index is a great place to start when day trading and forex trading. It has default settings of 14 periods and 70/30 levels. The RSI setting will affect how frequently the RSI gives overbought and oversold signals, as well as divergence signals to buy and sell.
block_03: The default RSI setting is 14-periods. That means the indicator is calculated using the last 14 candles or the last 14 bars on the price chart.
block_04: Using a shorter timeframe, for example, 5-periods, will cause the RSI to reach extreme values (above 70 or below 30) more often. By the same token, longer timeframe settings will see the RSI indicator reach above 70 or below 30 less frequently.
block_05: Most of the PRO traders use the unconventional approach of 60/40 RSI and range shift concept – a phenomenon that occurs when the RSI indicator shifts from a predefined range to another predefined range due to the change in the price of the stock.
block_06: There are different ranges for RSI Indicator, for example in a bullish market – 40/80, and in a bearish market – 65/30. This range shift concept can be applied in any timeframe, whether intraday, daily, weekly, etc.
block_07: Thus, the range shift concept helps you understand whether the market is bullish, bearish, or sideways.
block_10: Log in to your CapitalBear account and open the traderoom
block_11: Click the Indicator button in the bottom-left corner of the chart
block_12: Select Relative Strength Index from the Momentum section
block_13: Set up your indicator and apply it to the chart
block_16: Stay tuned for emails, so you don't miss the trading tips.
